sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports intermittent diarrhea and abdominal cramping, exacerbated by spicy foods and alcohol consumption. He notes increased frequency (2-3 times daily) and reports feeling better with water instead of soda. He also reports occasional nausea.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The abdomen is soft, non-tender, with normal active bowel sounds. No masses or organomegaly are palpated.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are consistent with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), likely triggered by dietary factors and alcohol.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe citrucel 2 tablets twice daily for regularity. Recommend bentyl 20mg as needed 30-45 minutes before eating out, and repeat up to four times daily. Start elavil 25mg at bedtime. Schedule a follow-up phone call in one month to assess response to treatment and address any side effects. Advise patient to reduce intake of greasy and fatty foods and alcohol.
